summary.para: Specifies the composite operation to be used by a Collider2D.
Indicates a composite operation that composes geometry using a Boolean NOT operation.
Indicates a composite operation that composes geometry using a Boolean XOR operation.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Collider2D.CreateMesh(System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)

summary.para: Creates a planar Mesh that is identical to the area defined by the Collider2D geometry.

member.param: Should the mesh be transformed by the position of the attached Rigidbody2D?
Should the mesh be transformed by the rotation of the attached Rigidbody2D?

When true, Delaunay triangulation is used to generate the mesh. This can reduce the number of vertices created in the Collider mesh and reduce the number of small triangle fans produced, both of which can improve overall mesh size and performance.

returns.para: The planar Mesh created that matches the collider geometry or NULL if no Mesh could be created.

This mode is obsolete. You should use Discrete mode.
A Collider that can merge other Colliders together.
Controls the radius of all edges created by the Collider.
Specifies when to generate the Composite Collider geometry.
Specifies the type of geometry the Composite Collider should generate.
Vertices are offset by this distance when compositing multiple physic shapes. Any vertices between shapes within this distance are combined.
The number of paths in the Collider.
Gets the total number of points in all the paths within the Collider.
When the value is true, the Collider uses an additional Delaunay triangulation step to produce the Collider mesh. When the value is false, this additional step does not occur.
Controls the minimum distance allowed between generated vertices.
Regenerates the Composite Collider geometry.
Specifies when to generate the Composite Collider geometry.

Sets the Composite Collider geometry to update synchronously immediately when a Collider used by the Composite Collider changes.
Specifies the type of geometry the Composite Collider generates.
Sets the Composite Collider 2D to generate closed outlines for the merged collider geometry consisting of only edges.
Sets the Composite Collider 2D to generate closed outlines for the merged collider geometry consisting of convex polygon shapes.

summary.para: Applies both linear and angular (torque) forces continuously to the rigidbody each physics update.
The linear force applied to the rigidbody each physics update.
The linear force, relative to the rigid-body coordinate system, applied each physics update.
The torque applied to the rigidbody each physics update.

A set of parameters for filtering contact results. Define the angle by referring to their position in world space, where 0 degrees is parallel to the positive x-axis, 90 degrees is parallel to the positive y-axis, 180 degrees is parallel to the negative x-axis, and 270 degrees is parallel to the negative y-axis.
Given the current state of the contact filter, determine whether it would filter anything.
Sets the contact filter to filter the results that only include Collider2D on the layers defined by the layer mask.
Sets the contact filter to filter the results to only include Collider2D with a Z coordinate (depth) less than this value.
Sets the contact filter to filter the results to only include contacts with collision normal angles that are less than this angle.
Sets the contact filter to filter the results to only include Collider2D with a Z coordinate (depth) greater than this value.
Sets the contact filter to filter the results to only include contacts with collision normal angles that are greater than this angle.
Returns a new contact filter with a state where it will not filter any contacts.
Sets the contact filter to filter the results by depth using minDepth and maxDepth.
Sets the contact filter to filter results by layer mask.
Sets the contact filter to filter the results by the collision's normal angle using minNormalAngle and maxNormalAngle.
Sets the contact filter to filter within the minDepth and maxDepth range, or outside that range.
Sets the contact filter to filter within the minNormalAngle and maxNormalAngle range, or outside that range.
Sets to filter contact results based on trigger collider involvement.
Turns off depth filtering by setting useDepth to false. The associated values of minDepth and maxDepth are not changed.
Turns off layer mask filtering by setting useLayerMask to false. The associated value of layerMask is not changed.
Turns off normal angle filtering by setting useNormalAngle to false. The associated values of minNormalAngle and maxNormalAngle are not changed.

Collider for 2D physics representing an arbitrary set of connected edges (lines) defined by its vertices.

Defines the position of a virtual point adjacent to the start point of the EdgeCollider2D.

Gets the number of points.
Get or set the points defining multiple continuous edges.

Set this to true to use the adjacentEndPoint to form the collision normal that is used to calculate the collision response when a collision occurs at the Edge Collider's end point. Set this to false to not use the adjacentEndPoint, and the collision normal becomes the direction of motion of the collision.

Set this to true to use the adjacentStartPoint to form the collision normal that is used to calculate the collision response when a collision occurs at the Edge Collider's start point. Set this to false to not use the adjacentStartPoint, and the collision normal becomes the direction of motion of the collision.

Options for selecting which action to take when a Joint2D breaks.

summary.para:
A body is contained within a world and has 3 degrees-of-freedom, two for position and one for rotation. A body can have forces, torques and impulses applied to it. A body has three distinct types:
- Static: This type of body does not move under simulation and behaves as if it has infinite mass, essentially an immovable object. Static bodies never interact with other Static or Kinematic bodies. - Dynamic: This type of body is fully simulated and moves according to forces and torques applied to its linearangular velocities. It can interact with all other body types. It always has finite, non-zero mass. - Kinematic: This type of body moves under simulation and moves according to its linearangular velocities and never uses forces or torques. It only interacts with Dynamic body types. It behaves as if it has infinite mass.
A body is automatically destroyed when the world it is in is destroyed. A body cannot exist outside a world.
The angular damping of the body. This will reduce the angular velocity over time. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.angularVelocity.
The angular velocity of the body.
The awake state of the body.

Get/Set the System.Object that event callbacks for this body will be sent to. Care should be taken with any System.Object assigned as a callback target that isn't a UnityEngine.Object as this assignment will not in itself keep the object alive and can be garbage collected. To avoid this, you should have at least a single reference to the object in your code.
This includes the following events:
- A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.BodyUpdateEvent with call LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IBodyUpdateCallback.
Get/Set the degrees of freedom constraints (locks) for the body of Linear X, Linear Y and Rotation Z.

Get/Set a body definition by accessing all of its current properties. This is provided as convenience only and should not be used when performance is important as all the properties defined in the definition are accessed sequentially. You should try to only use the specific properties you need rather than using this feature.
The enabled state of the body. If false, the body and anything attached to it will not participate in the simulation.

Treat this body as high speed object that performs continuous collision detection against dynamic and kinematic bodies, but not other high speed bodies. Fast collision bodies should be used sparingly. They are not a solution for general dynamic-versus-dynamic continuous collision.
This allows this body to bypass rotational speed limits. This should only be used for circular objects, such as wheels, balls etc.

Scales the world gravity that is applied to this body. Setting the gravity scale to zero stops any gravity being applied. Likewise, a negative value inverts gravity. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.gravity.
Get if the body is owned. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.SetOwner.
Checks if a body is valid.
Get the number of joints attached to this body. Use LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.GetJoints to retrieve the joints.
The linear damping of the body. This will reduce the linear velocity over time. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.linearVelocity.
The linear velocity of the body.
The center of mass position of the body in local space.
The calculated mass of the body, usually in kilograms. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.massConfiguration.

The body mass configuration. Normally this is computed automatically using the shape geometry and density. This information changes if a shape is added or removed or if the body type changes. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.MassConfiguration.
Get LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose, typically by the owner only.
The position of the body in the world.
The rotation of the body.
The rotational inertia of the body, usually in kg*m^2. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.massConfiguration.
Get the number of shapes attached to this body. Use LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.GetShapes to retrieve the shapes.
The sleeping ability of the body. If false, the body will never sleep and will be woken up. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.awake.
The threshold below which the body will sleep, usually in m/s.
The full transform of the body composed of position and rotation.
GetSet the transform object associated with the body. This can be used as a write transform andor as a hint for debug drawing. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.transformWriteMode.

Get/Set how the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.transformObject should be written to after the simulation has completed. Transform write will only occur if it is enabled on the world using LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.transformWriteMode.
A body is one of these three body types, Dynamic, Kinematic or Static, each of which determines how the body behaves in the simulation.
Get/Set LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose. The physics system doesn't use this data, it is entirely for custom use.
Get the world the body is attached to.
The center of mass position of the body in world space.

summary.para: Apply an angular impulse. This should be used for one-shot impulses. If you need a steady torque, use a torque instead, which will work better with the sub-stepping solver.

member.param: The angular impulse, usually in units of kg"m"m/s.
Should the body be woken up.

The world position of the point of application.
Should the body be woken up.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.ApplyLinearImpulse(UnityEngine.Vector2,UnityEngine.Vector2,System.Boolean)

summary.para: Apply an impulse at a point. This immediately modifies the velocity and also modifies the angular velocity if the point of application is not at the center of mass. This should be used for one-shot impulses. If you need a steady force, use a force instead, which will work better with the sub-stepping solver.

member.param: The world impulse vector, usually in N"s or kg"m/s.
The world position of the point of application.
Should the body be woken up.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.ApplyLinearImpulseToCenter(UnityEngine.Vector2,System.Boolean)

summary.para: Apply an impulse to the center of mass. This immediately modifies the velocity. This should be used for one-shot impulses. If you need a steady force, use a force instead, which will work better with the sub-stepping solver.

member.param: The world impulse vector, usually in N"s or kg"m/s.
Should the body be woken up.

summary.para: This updates the mass configuration to the sum of the mass configuration of all the attached shapes. This normally does not need to be called unless you set the mass configuration to override the mass and you later want to reset the mass. You should call this regardless of body type. Note that sensor shapes may have mass.

summary.para: Body constrains constrain the degrees of freedom a body when solving the simulation.
Constrain rotation and motion along all axes.
No constraints
Constrain motion along all axes.
Constrain motion along the X-axis.
Constrain motion along the Y-axis.
FreConstraineze rotation along the Z-axis.
A body is one of these three body types, Dynamic, Kinematic or Static, each of which determines how the body behaves in the simulation.
A dynamic body has positive mass, velocity determined by forces and is moved by solver.
A kinematic body has zero mass, velocity set by user and is moved by solver
A static body has zero mass, zero velocity and may be manually moved.

Clear any user forces that have been applied to this body. Forces on a body are automatically cleared when a simulation step completes, however under some circumstances it may be desirable to clear the forces explicitly.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.GetPositionAndRotation3D(UnityEngine.Transform,UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld/TransformWriteMode,UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld/TransformPlane,UnityEngine.Vector3&,UnityEngine.Quaternion&)

summary.para: Get the full 3D position and rotation of the body given the specified LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.TransformWriteMode and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.TransformPlane. Usually both the write-mode and transform-plane of the world the body is in would be used. This can only be called when a LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.transformObject is assigned. Without this, an exception is thrown. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.transformWriteMode and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.transformPlane.

member.param: The Transform object to be used as a reference when converting from 2D positionrotation to 3D positionrotation, usually the same as the TransformObject assigned to the PhysicsBody.
The transform write mode to use, usually the one currently assigned to the world is used.
The transform plane to use, usually the one currently assigned to the world is used.
The calculated output position.
The calculated output rotation.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.SetBatchForce(System.ReadOnlySpan`1<UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody/BatchForce>)

summary.para: Apply a force for a batch of LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody using a span of LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.BatchForce. If invalid values are passed to the batch, they will simply be ignored. For best performance, the bodies contained in the batch should all be part of the same LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld. If the bodies in the batch are not contained in the same LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld, the batch should be sorted by the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld the bodies are contained within.

member.param: The batch of bodies and values to set.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.SetOwner(UnityEngine.Object)

summary.para: Set the (optional) owner object associated with this body and return an owner key that must be specified when destroying the body with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.Destroy. The physics system provides access to all objects, including the ability to destroy them so this feature can be used to stop accidental destruction of objects that are owned by other objects. You can only set the owner once, multiple attempts will produce a warning. The lifetime of the specified owner object is not linked to this body i.e. this body will still be owned by the owner object, even if it is destroyed. It is also valid to not specify an owner object (NULL) to simply gain an owner key however it can be useful, if simply for debugging purposes and discovery, to know which object is the owner.

member.param: The object that owns this body. This can be NULL if not required.

returns.para: An owner key that must be passed to LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.Destroy when destroying the body.

member.param: The user data to set.
Optional owner key returned when using PhysicsBody.SetOwner.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.SetTransformTarget(UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsTransform,System.Single)

summary.para: Set the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.linearVelocity and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.angularVelocity to reach the specified transform in the specified time. The resultant transform will be closed by may not be exact. This is designed ideally for Kinematic bodies but will work with Dynamic bodies if nothing changes the assigned velocities. This will be ignored if the calculated LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.linearVelocity and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.angularVelocity would be below the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.sleepThreshold. This will automatically wake the body if it is asleep.

member.param: The transform target for the body.
The timer over which to calculate the required velocities to move to the transform.

summary.para: The method used to Write the body pose to the Transform. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.transformWriteMode.
The current body pose will be written to the Transform.
The pose extrapolated from the current body pose to a future pose based upon the current linear/angular velocities will be written to the Transform. The transform pose is essentially predictive.
The interpolated pose from the previous body pose to the current body pose will be written to the Transform. The transform pose is essentially historic.
This body pose won't be written to the Transform.
Used to define a Transform write "tween" for a body.
The angular velocity of the body to be used during the lifetime of the tween, in degrees per second.
The body to be used during the lifetime of the tween.
The linear velocity of the body to be used during the lifetime of the tween.
The physics transform to be used during the lifetime of the tween.
The start position of the Transform tween. See UnityEngine.Transform.position.
The start rotation of the Transform. See UnityEngine.Transform.rotation.

The transform write mode to be used during the lifetime of the tween. Anything other than LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.TransformWriteMode.Interpolate or LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.TransformWriteMode.Extrapolate will be removed.
Wake any bodies that are touching this body via their shapes. This also works for Static bodies.
A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody definition used to specify important initial properties.

Angular damping is used to reduce the angular velocity over time i.e. slow down rotating bodies. The damping parameter can be larger than 1.0f but the damping effect becomes sensitive to the time step when the damping parameter is large.
The initial angular velocity of the body, in degrees per second.
Is this body initially awake or sleeping?
The degrees of freedom constraints (locks) for the body of Linear X, Linear Y and Rotation Z.
Get a default LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody definition.
Used to disable a body. A disabled body does not move or collide.

Treat this body as high speed object that performs continuous collision detection against dynamic and kinematic bodies, but not other high speed bodies. Fast collision bodies should be used sparingly. They are not a solution for general dynamic-versus-dynamic continuous collision.
This allows this body to bypass rotational speed limits. This should only be used for circular objects, such as wheels, balls etc.
Scale the gravity applied to this body, non-dimensional.

Linear damping is use to reduce the linear velocity i.e. slow down translating bodies. The damping parameter can be larger than 1 but the damping effect becomes sensitive to the time step when the damping parameter is large. Generally linear damping is undesirable because it makes objects move slowly as if they are floating.
The initial linear velocity of the body's origin, in meters/sec.

The initial position of the body, in world-space. Bodies should be created with the desired position as creating bodies at the origin and then moving them nearly doubles the cost of body creation, especially if the body is moved after shapes have been added.

The initial rotation of the body, in world-space. Bodies should be created with the desired rotation as creating bodies at the origin and then rotating them nearly doubles the cost of body creation, especially if the body is moved after shapes have been added.
Set this flag to false if this body should never fall asleep.
A speed threshold below which the body is allowed to sleep.
The method used to Write the body pose to the Transform.
A body is one of these three body types, Dynamic, Kinematic or Static, each of which determines how the body behaves in the simulation.
Create a default LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody definition.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IContactFilterCallback.OnContactFilter2D(UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents/ContactFilterEvent)

summary.para:
Called when a pair of shapes are determined to be in contact. This is called to decide if a contact will be created for these shapes, allowing contact creation to be bypassed so a contact will not go to the solver. This is only called if the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld has LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.contactFilterCallbacks set to true. An event is only produced if one of the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape have LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.contactFilterCallbacks set to true. This is called for both triggers and non-triggers but only with Dynamic bodies.
Extreme care must be taken with this callback!!
This callback occurs during the simulation step and can be called from any thread, therefore it must be thread-safe. During this time, the simulation state is undefined for the broadphase, events etc. For this reason, any attempt to perform a write operation will result in a deadlock as the world itself is write locked. Performing simple read operations on LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody, LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape or LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint is safe, such as reading velocity or getting the geometry of a shape however, more complex operations involving the world such as performing a query can result in corruption or crashes. A recommendation is reading UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData from any object which is a completely safe read operation therefore any required information should be encoded there if possible.

member.param: The event that occurred.

returns.para: Return false if you do not want a contact to be created during this simulation step. Returning true allows the contact to be created.

summary.para: An interface that when implemented by a UnityEngine.Object, can be called as a target by LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SendJointThresholdCallbacks.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IJointThresholdCallback.OnJointThreshold2D(UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents/JointThresholdEvent)

summary.para: Called when a LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.JointThresholdEvent for the object occurs. This will always be called on the main-thread after the simulation has finished.

member.param: The event that occurred.

summary.para: An interface that when implemented by a UnityEngine.Object, can be called as a target when a LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape has LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.preSolveCallbacks set to true. The LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape is in also has to have its LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.preSolveCallbacks set to true.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IPreSolveCallback.OnPreSolve2D(UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents/PreSolveEvent)

summary.para:
Called when a contact between a pair of shapes is updated. This allows a contact to be disabled before it goes to the solver. A typical use-case would be to implement a one-way behaviour based upon the provided contact. This is only called if the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld has LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.preSolveCallbacks set to true. An event is only produced if one of the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape have LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.preSolveCallbacks set to true. This is only called for Awake Dynamic bodies. This is not called for triggers.
Extreme care must be taken with this callback!!
This callback occurs during the simulation step and can be called from any thread, therefore it must be thread-safe. During this time, the simulation state is undefined for the broadphase, events etc. For this reason, any attempt to perform a write operation will result in a deadlock as the world itself is write locked. Performing simple read operations on LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody, LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape or LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint is safe, such as reading velocity or getting the geometry of a shape, however more complex operations involving the world such as performing a query can result in corruption or crashes. A recommend is using the provided contact details to make a decision in the callback. An additional recommendation is reading UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData from any object which is a completely safe read operation therefore any required information should be encoded there if possible.

member.param: The event that occurred.

returns.para: Return false if you want to disable the contact this simulation step. Returning true allows the contact.

The event.
Contains all the trigger callback targets returned from LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.GetTriggerCallbackTargets.

Trigger begin event target for callbacks.

The callback target (LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.callbackTarget) associated with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.TriggerBeginEvent.triggerShape. This returns any implemented LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.ITriggerCallback or NULL if not implemented or no target.

The callback target (LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.callbackTarget) associated with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.TriggerBeginEvent.visitorShape. This returns any implemented LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.ITriggerCallback or NULL if not implemented or no target.

The trigger end event.

The callback target (LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.callbackTarget) associated with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.TriggerEndEvent.triggerShape. This returns any implemented LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.ITriggerCallback or NULL if not implemented or no target.

The callback target (LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.callbackTarget) associated with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.TriggerEndEvent.visitorShape. This returns any implemented LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.ITriggerCallback or NULL if not implemented or no target.

A dedicated shape that produces a chain of shapes connected together to produce a continuous surface. Chain shapes provide a smooth, continuous surface that will not produce "ghost" collisions. A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChain is automatically destroyed when the body it is in is destroyed. A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChain cannot exist unattached from a body.
This will produce shapes of type LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.ShapeType.ChainSegment.
- Chains are one-sided. - A chain has no mass and should be used on static bodies. - A chain can have a counter-clockwise winding order (normal points right of segment direction). - A chain is either a loop or open. - A chain must have at least 4 points. - The distance between any two points must be greater than LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.linearSlop. - A chain should not self intersect (this is not validated). - An open chain has no collision on the first and final edge. - You may overlap two open chains on their first three and/or last three points to get smooth collision.

Get the world AABB that bounds this chain. The bounds of the shape is inflated slightly due to speculative collision detection. The inflation is smaller on Static shape types however it is not zero due to time-of-impact collision detection. If an exact AABB is required then you can retrieve that via the shape geometry.
The body which the chain is attached to.
The bounciness of the chain. Usually this is within the range [0, 1]. Values higher than 1 will result in energy being added which can lead to an unstable simulation.
Defines the method used when mixing the friction values of two shapes to form a shape contact. This is assigned to the current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.surfaceMaterial.

Get/Set the System.Object that callbacks for the shapes owned by this chain will be sent to. Care should be taken with any System.Object assigned as a callback target that isn't a UnityEngine.Object as this assignment will not in itself keep the object alive and can be garbage collected. To avoid this, you should have at least a single reference to the object in your code.
This includes the following events:
- A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.ContactFilterEvent with call LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IContactFilterCallback. - A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.PreSolveEvent with call LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IPreSolveCallback. - A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.TriggerBeginEvent with call LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.ITriggerCallback. - A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.TriggerEndEvent with call LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.ITriggerCallback. - A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.ContactBeginEvent with call LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IContactCallback. - A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.ContactEndEvent with call LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IContactCallback.
The friction of the owned chain shapes. Usually this is within the range [0, 1]. Values higher than 1 will result in energy being added which can lead to an unstable simulation.
Defines the method used when mixing the friction values of two shapes to form a shape contact. This is assigned to the current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.surfaceMaterial.
Get if the chain is owned. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChain.SetOwner.
Check if the shape is valid.
Get LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose, typically by the owner only.
Get the number of Chain segments that this chain has created and owns. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.ShapeType.ChainSegment.
Get/Set LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose. The physics system doesn't use this data, it is entirely for custom use.
Get the world the chain is attached to.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChain.Destroy(System.Int32)

summary.para: Destroy the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChain and all the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.ShapeType.ChainSegment it owns. If the object is owned with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChain.SetOwner then you must provide the owner key it returned. Failing to do so will return a warning and the chain will not be destroyed. The lifetime of the specified owner object is not linked to this chain i.e. this chain will still be owned by the owner object, even if it is destroyed. This is the only way to destroy shapes of type LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.ShapeType.ChainSegment if they were created by a LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChain.

member.param: Optional owner key returned when using LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChain.SetOwner.

returns.para: If the chain was destroyed or not.

summary.para: Get the owner object associated with this chain as specified using LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChain.SetOwner.

returns.para: The owner object associated with this chain or NULL if no owner has been specified.

summary.para: Get the index of the specified Chain Segment PhysicsShape.

member.param: The chain segment shape to find the index of.

returns.para: The index of the chain segment shape in its parent chain. This is a value of zero to the number of chain segment shapes - 1.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChain.GetSegments(Unity.Collections.Allocator)

summary.para: Get all the Chain segments that this chain has created and owns.

member.param: The memory allocator to use for the results. This can only be Unity.Collections.Allocator.Temp, Unity.Collections.Allocator.TempJob or Unity.Collections.Allocator.Persistent.

returns.para: The chain segments that this chain has created and owns. This NativeArray must be disposed of after use otherwise leaks will occur. The exception to this is if the array is empty.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChain.SetOwner(UnityEngine.Object)

summary.para: Set the (optional) owner object associated with this chain and return an owner key that must be specified when destroying the shape with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChain.Destroy. The physics system provides access to all objects, including the ability to destroy them so this feature can be used to stop accidental destruction of objects that are owned by other objects. You can only set the owner once, multiple attempts will produce a warning. It is also valid to not specify an owner object (NULL) to simply gain an owner key however it can be useful, if simply for debugging purposes and discovery, to know which object is the owner.

member.param: The object that owns this chain. This can be NULL if not required.

returns.para: An owner key that must be passed to LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChain.Destroy when destroying the chain.

Check if a Physics Composer is valid.
Get the number of layers currently added to the Physics Composer.
Get the layer handles added to the Physics Composer.

summary.para: Get/Set the maximum number of polygon vertices to be used when composing polygon output. This should be in the range of 3 to UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsConstants.MaxPolygonVertices. The default is UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsConstants.MaxPolygonVertices.

Get the number of geometries that were rejected during the last Geometry Composition. Geometry can be rejected for a number of reasons such as vertices being collinear or too close etc. Whilst "pure" geometry is always valid, this geometry is meant to be used by physics which has constraints on what it can accept. All geometry successfully created will always be valid when used by physics. If you notice thin/small gaps in the composition, this is likely to be rejected geometry. Checking this property will help determine that.
Get/Set if Delaunay tessellation is to be used.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsComposer.Create(Unity.Collections.Allocator)

summary.para:
Create a Physics Composer.
NOTE: The composer implements System.IDisposable which allows you to use the "using" statement on the returned composer object to ensure it's disposed in the current scope which is only useful when using the Unity.Collections.Allocator.Temp allocator. However, because the composer is a struct, System.IDisposable will not be called automatically so in this case disposing or simply calling PhysicsComposer.Destroy must be done explicitly.

member.param: The memory allocator to use when adding layers. It is not used to create the composer itself which must be destroyed. This can only be Unity.Collections.Allocator.Temp, Unity.Collections.Allocator.TempJob or Unity.Collections.Allocator.Persistent.

returns.para: The new Physics Composer.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsComposer.GetGeometryIslands(Unity.Collections.Allocator)

summary.para: Get the geometry islands from a previous polygon geometry composition i.e. a call to LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsComposer.CreatePolygonGeometry or LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsComposer.CreateConvexHulls. Each generated polygon or convex-hull belongs to a unique island defining a set of polygons that are connected together as they share edges. The array returned contains a series of ranges where each range is a unique connected island where the range indicates both the start index and length of the original polygon indices. The number of discovered unique islands is defined by the size of the returned array.

member.param: The memory allocator to use for the results. This can only be Unity.Collections.Allocator.Temp, Unity.Collections.Allocator.TempJob or Unity.Collections.Allocator.Persistent.

returns.para: A NativeArray containing a series of ranges where each range is a uniquely connected island where the range indicates both the start and end indices of the original polygon indices.

summary.para: A composer layer handle.

A composer operation.

Perform a NOT operation (geometric difference).
Perform an OR operation (geometric merge).
Perform an XOR operation (geometric flip).

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor.Fragment(UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor/FragmentGeometry,System.ReadOnlySpan`1<UnityEngine.Vector2>,Unity.Collections.Allocator)

summary.para:
Fragment the specified target geometry using the specified fragment points.
The fragment points define areas where polygon fragments will be produced from the target geometry.
If the resulting polygon fragments have more polygon vertices than can fit into a single LowLevelPhysics2D.PolygonGeometry then the fragment will be split into multiple polygon fragments. The maximum number of vertices a single polygon fragment can have is defined by LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsConstants.MaxPolygonVertices.
If even a single fragment point overlaps the target geometry then all results will be returned in LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor.FragmentResult.brokenGeometry. If none of the fragment points overlap the target geometry then all the results will be returned in LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor.FragmentResult.unbrokenGeometry. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor.FragmentResult.

member.param: The target geometry to fragment. There must be at least a single geometry element. Any target polygons with a non-zero radius will be ignored.
The world-space fragment points used to define fragment regions. The number of fragment points must be greater than 1.
The memory allocator to use for the results. This can only be Unity.Collections.Allocator.Temp, Unity.Collections.Allocator.TempJob or Unity.Collections.Allocator.Persistent.

returns.para: The fragment results. These results must be disposed of after use otherwise leaks will occur.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor.Fragment(UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor/FragmentGeometry,UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor/FragmentGeometry,System.ReadOnlySpan`1<UnityEngine.Vector2>,Unity.Collections.Allocator)

summary.para:
Fragment the specified mask geometry using the specified fragment points, after the target geometry has the mask (carving) geometry removed from it.
The target geometry is first clipped with the mask geometry using a LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsComposer.Operation.NOT operation. The resulting target geometry is returned in LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor.FragmentResult.unbrokenGeometry. The mask geometry is then clipped with the original target geometry using an LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsComposer.Operation.AND operation. If the clipped mask produces no geometry then no results are returned in LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor.FragmentResult.brokenGeometry.
The fragment points define areas where polygon fragments will be produced from the clipped masked geometry. The resulting polygon fragments are returned in LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor.FragmentResult.brokenGeometry.
If the resulting polygon fragments have more polygon vertices than can fit into a single LowLevelPhysics2D.PolygonGeometry then the fragment will be split into multiple polygon fragments. The maximum number of vertices a single polygon fragment can have is defined by LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsConstants.MaxPolygonVertices. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor.FragmentResult.

member.param: The target geometry to fragment. There must be at least a single geometry element. Any target polygons with a non-zero radius will be ignored.
The mask geometry that will be used to clip the target geometry. There must be at least a single geometry element. Any mask polygons with a non-zero radius will be ignored.
The world-space fragment points used to define fragment regions. The number of fragment points must be greater than 1.
The memory allocator to use for the results. This can only be Unity.Collections.Allocator.Temp, Unity.Collections.Allocator.TempJob or Unity.Collections.Allocator.Persistent.

returns.para: The fragment results. The transform returned here is the one provided in the target geometry. These results must be disposed of after use otherwise leaks will occur.

summary.para: The polygon geometry used when fragmenting.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor.FragmentGeometry.#ctor(UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsTransform,System.ReadOnlySpan`1<UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PolygonGeometry>)

summary.para: Create fragment geometry.

member.param: The transform used to transform the specified Polygon geometry.
The Polygon geometry to use.

summary.para: The result of a fragment operation. This must be disposed of after use otherwise leaks will occur. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor.Fragment.
The geometry that was fragmented (broken).
The transform used when fragmenting. All returned geometry uses this.
The geometry that was not fragmented (unbroken).

The geometry islands indicating how polygons are connected together. Each generated polygon belongs to a unique island defining a set of polygons that are connected together as they share edges. The array returned contains a series of ranges where each range is a unique connected island where the range indicates both the start index and length of the original polygon indices. The number of discovered unique islands is defined by the size of the returned array.
This is only valid when fragmenting with a mask with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor.Fragment.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor.Slice(UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor/FragmentGeometry,UnityEngine.Vector2,UnityEngine.Vector2,Unity.Collections.Allocator)

summary.para:
Slice the specified target geometry using the specified slice line.
The target geometry is sliced using the specified ray as defined by and . The specified line segment and are extended to infinity and so defines a 2D intersection plane.
All valid target geometry will returned in either the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor.SliceResult.leftGeometry or LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor.SliceResult.rightGeometry depending on its side of the line (sliced or not). Left and Right are defined as "looking" along the ray in the direction defined by with Left being anything to the left of the ray and Right being anything to the right of the ray. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor.SliceResult.

member.param: The target geometry to slice. There must be at least a single geometry element. Any target polygons with a non-zero radius will be ignored.
The start of the ray slice line.
The translation relative to the origin of the slice ray. This must have a non-zero magnitude.
The memory allocator to use for the results. This can only be Unity.Collections.Allocator.Temp, Unity.Collections.Allocator.TempJob or Unity.Collections.Allocator.Persistent.

returns.para: The slice results. The transform returned here is the one provided in the target geometry. These results must be disposed of after use otherwise leaks will occur.

summary.para: The result of a slice operation. This must be disposed of after use otherwise leaks will occur. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDestructor.Slice.
The geometry that was sliced on the "left" of the line.
The geometry that was sliced on the "right" of the line.
The transform used when slicing. All returned geometry uses this.
Dispose of the fragment result.

Get/Set the System.Object that event callbacks for this joint will be sent to. Care should be taken with any System.Object assigned as a callback target that isn't a UnityEngine.Object as this assignment will not in itself keep the object alive and can be garbage collected. To avoid this, you should have at least a single reference to the object in your code.
This includes the following events:
- A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.JointThresholdEvent with call LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IJointThresholdCallback.
Whether the shapes on the pair of bodies can come into contact.

Get the current constraint torque used by the joint, usually in newtons.
Get the current distance.
Get the current linear separation error for this joint, usually in meters. This does not consider admissible movement.
The current motor force, usually in newtons.
The desired distance constraint i.e. the rest length of this joint. This has a lower stable limit of just above zero.
Controls the scaling of the joint drawing.

Enable/Disable the joint motor.
Enable/Disable the spring behaviour. If false then the joint will be rigid, overriding the limit and motor.
The force threshold beyond which a joint event will be produced.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDistanceJoint.SetOwner(UnityEngine.Object)

summary.para: Set the (optional) owner object associated with this joint and return an owner key that must be specified when destroying the joint with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.Destroy. The physics system provides access to all objects, including the ability to destroy them so this feature can be used to stop accidental destruction of objects that are owned by other objects. You can only set the owner once, multiple attempts will produce a warning. The lifetime of the specified owner object is not linked to this joint i.e. this joint will still be owned by the owner object, even if it is destroyed. It is also valid to not specify an owner object (NULL) to simply gain an owner key however it can be useful, if simply for debugging purposes and discovery, to know which object is the owner.

member.param: The object that owns this joint. This can be NULL if not required.

returns.para: An owner key that must be passed to LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.Destroy when destroying the joint.

member.param: The user data to set.
Optional owner key returned when using PhysicsJoint.SetOwner.

Enable/Disable the distance constraint to behave like a spring. If false then the distance joint will be rigid, overriding the limit and motor.

The desired motor speed, usually in meters per second.

The lower spring force controls how much tension the spring can sustain.
The upper spring force controls how much compression the spring can sustain.
The torque threshold beyond which a joint event will be produced.
Controls the joint stiffness damping, non-dimensional. Use 1 for critical damping.
Controls the joint stiffness frequency, in cycles per second.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsDistanceJointDefinition.#ctor(System.Boolean)

summary.para: Various events that can be retrieved during and after the simulation has completed. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Simulate and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Simulate.

An event produced by a LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody that indicates the simulation changed the body in one of the following ways:
- The body transform was changed. - The body fell asleep. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.bodyUpdateEvents.
The body this event relates to.
Whether the body fell asleep or not.
The current transform of the body.

An event produced by a pair of Shapes, neither of which are a trigger, began touching. The shapes provided may have been destroyed so they should always be validated with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.isValid. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.contactBeginEvents.

The unique Id of the contact. This contact is volatile and may be destroyed automatically when the world is modified or simulated therefore it should always be checked for validity with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.ContactId.isValid.
One of the shapes involved in the event.
The other shape involved in the event.

An event produced by a pair of Shapes, neither of which are a trigger, stopped touching. You will get an end event if you do anything that destroys contacts prior to the last world simulation step which include things like setting the body transform, destroying a body etc. The shapes provided may have been destroyed so they should always be validated with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.isValid. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.contactEndEvents.

The unique Id of the contact. This contact is volatile and may be destroyed automatically when the world is modified or simulated therefore it should always be checked for validity with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.ContactId.isValid.
One of the shapes involved in the event.
The other shape involved in the event.
An event produced when a pair of LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape come into contact. This can be used to decide if a contact between the two shapes should be created or not.
The physics world both shapes are within.
One of the shapes involved in the event.
The other shape involved in the event.

An event produced when a pair of LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape come into contact at relative speed exceeding the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.contactHitEventThreshold.
The shapes provided may have been destroyed so they should always be validated with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.isValid. This may be reported for speculative contacts that have a confirmed impulse. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.contactHitEvents.
The speed the shapes are approaching, typically in meters per second. This value is always positive.

The unique Id of the contact. This contact is volatile and may be destroyed automatically when the world is modified or simulated therefore it should always be checked for validity with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.ContactId.isValid.
Normal vector that always points in the direction from shape A to shape B.

Point where the shapes hit at the beginning of the time step. This is a mid-point between the two surfaces. It could be at speculative point where the two shapes were not touching at the beginning of the time step.
One of the shapes involved in the event.
The other shape involved in the event.
An event produced by a Joint which exceeds either its LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.forceThreshold or LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.torqueThreshold.
The joint involved in the event.

Event callback for a post-simulate event. This is called after the simulation has finished running and is always called on the main-thread. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.PostSimulateEventHandler.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.PostSimulateEventHandler

summary.para: Event handler for a post-simulate event callback. This is called after the simulation has finished running and is always called on the main-thread. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.PostSimulate.

member.param: The world that has just been simulated.
The amount of time the world was forward simulated.

summary.para: Event callback for a pre-simulate event. This is called prior to the simulation running and is always called on the main-thread. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.PreSimulateEventHandler.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.PreSimulateEventHandler

summary.para: Event handler for a pre-simulate event callback. This is called prior to the simulation running and is always called on the main-thread. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.PreSimulate.

member.param: The world which is about to be simulated.
The amount of time the world will be forward simulated.

summary.para: An event produced when a contact between a pair of LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape is updated, used to provide the ability to decide if the contact should be disabled or not.
The surface normal at the point of contact.
The physics world both shapes are within.
The point of contact.
One of the shapes involved in the event.
The other shape involved in the event.

An event produced when a pair of Shapes, one of which was a trigger, began touching. The shapes provided may have been destroyed so they should always be validated with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.isValid. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.triggerBeginEvents.
The trigger shape involved in the event.
The shape that began touching the trigger shape.

An event produced when a pair of Shapes, one of which was a trigger, stopped touching. An end event will be produced anything that destroys contacts happens, prior to the last world simulation step, which include things like setting the body transform, destroying a body or shape or changing a contact filter etc. The shapes provided may have been destroyed so they should always be validated with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.isValid. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.triggerEndEvents.
The trigger shape involved in the event.
The shape that stopped touching the trigger shape.

A joint to constrain a pair of bodies together rigidly. This constraint provides springs to mimic soft-body simulation. The approximate solver cannot always hold many bodies together completely rigidly.
Angular damping, non-dimensional. Use 1 for critical damping.
Angular stiffness in cycles per second. Use zero for maximum stiffness.
The second body the joint constrains.
A local anchor point on the first body for the constraint.

Get/Set the System.Object that event callbacks for this joint will be sent to. Care should be taken with any System.Object assigned as a callback target that isn't a UnityEngine.Object as this assignment will not in itself keep the object alive and can be garbage collected. To avoid this, you should have at least a single reference to the object in your code.
This includes the following events:
- A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.JointThresholdEvent with call LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IJointThresholdCallback.
Whether the shapes on the pair of bodies can come into contact.
Get the current angular separation error for this joint, in degrees. This does not consider admissible movement.
Get the current constraint force used by the joint, usually in newtons.
Get the current constraint torque used by the joint, usually in newtons.
Get the current linear separation error for this joint, usually in meters. This does not consider admissible movement.
Controls the scaling of the joint drawing.
The force threshold beyond which a joint event will be produced.
Get if the joint is owned. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.SetOwner.
Checks if the joint is valid.
Gets the joint type. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.JointType.
Linear damping, non-dimensional. Use 1 for critical damping.
Linear stiffness in cycles per second. Use zero for maximum stiffness.
The local anchor frame constraint relative to bodyA's origin.
The local anchor frame constraint relative to bodyB's origin.
Get LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose, typically by the owner only.
The torque threshold beyond which a joint event will be produced.
Controls the joint stiffness damping, non-dimensional. Use 1 for critical damping.
Controls the joint stiffness frequency, in cycles per second.
Get/Set LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose. The physics system doesn't use this data, it is entirely for custom use.
Get the world the body is attached to.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsIgnoreJoint.SetOwner(UnityEngine.Object)

summary.para: Set the (optional) owner object associated with this joint and return an owner key that must be specified when destroying the joint with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.Destroy. The physics system provides access to all objects, including the ability to destroy them so this feature can be used to stop accidental destruction of objects that are owned by other objects. You can only set the owner once, multiple attempts will produce a warning. The lifetime of the specified owner object is not linked to this joint i.e. this joint will still be owned by the owner object, even if it is destroyed. It is also valid to not specify an owner object (NULL) to simply gain an owner key however it can be useful, if simply for debugging purposes and discovery, to know which object is the owner.

member.param: The object that owns this joint. This can be NULL if not required.

returns.para: An owner key that must be passed to LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.Destroy when destroying the joint.

A joint is used to constrain bodies to the world or to each other in various ways. A joint is automatically destroyed when either body it is attached to is destroyed. A joint cannot exist unattached from a body.
The second body the joint constrains.
A local anchor point on the first body for the constraint.

Get/Set the System.Object object that event callbacks for this joint will be sent to. Care should be taken with any System.Object assigned as a callback target that isn't a UnityEngine.Object as this assignment will not in itself keep the object alive and can be garbage collected. To avoid this, you should have at least a single reference to the object in your code.
This includes the following events:
- A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.JointThresholdEvent with call LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IJointThresholdCallback.
Whether the shapes on the pair of bodies can come into contact.
Get the current angular separation error for this joint, in degrees. This does not consider admissible movement.
Get the current constraint force used by the joint, usually in newtons.
Get the current constraint torque used by the joint, usually in newtons.
Get the current linear separation error for this joint, usually in meters. This does not consider admissible movement.
Controls the scaling of the joint drawing. Not all joints have scalable elements but those that do will use this scaling.
The force threshold beyond which a joint event will be produced.
Get if the joint is owned. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.SetOwner.
Checks if the joint is valid.
Gets the joint type. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.JointType.
The local anchor frame constraint relative to bodyA's origin.
The local anchor frame constraint relative to bodyB's origin.
Get LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose, typically by the owner only.
The torque threshold beyond which a joint event will be produced.
Controls the joint stiffness damping, non-dimensional. Use 1 for critical damping.
Controls the joint stiffness frequency, in cycles per second.
Get/Set LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose. The physics system doesn't use this data, it is entirely for custom use.
Get the world the body is attached to.

This provides a common method to retrieving layer information. If a LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsLowLevelSettings2D asset is assigned then the full layers (LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsLowLevelSettings2D.physicsLayerNames) will be used if LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsLowLevelSettings2D.useFullLayers is also active. If no LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsLowLevelSettings2D asset is assigned then the global layers (See UnityEngine.LayerMask) will be used.

Physics LowLevel Settings Asset. This contains all the global physics options along with the default values for the following definitions:
- LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorldDefinition - LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBodyDefinition - LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShapeDefinition - LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChainDefinition

Controls the simulation and rendering of any LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld. When true, no automatic simulation or rendering will occur (bypassed). When false, normal operation occurs with automatic simulation and rendering. The only case for this to be true is when the low-level physics is not being used at all so this would remove any overhead of simulation or rendering but in most cases, this should be false which is the default.

Controls how many simulations can be started in parallel. Each one is started on its own worker and acts as its own main-thread. Workers should ideally be left free for the solver otherwise it may degrade solving performance. The actual quantity of workers used will always be capped to those available on the current device.
If the total number of workers available is below 4 then parallel simulation won't occur however parallel solving using workers will. This should not be confused with the quantity of workers used when solving a simulation. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorldDefinition.simulationWorkers.
Controls if the debug drawer can be used in a Player Development Build.

The internal length units per meter.
The physics system bases all length units on meters but you may need different units for your project. You can set this value to use different units but it should only be modified before any other calls to the physics system occur and only modified once. Changing this value after any physics object has been created can result in severe simulation instabilities.
Essentially there are some internal tolerances, such as how close two shapes need to be before they are considered to be touching or when two vertices of a hull are so close that they should be considered the same point. For example, internally a value of 5mm (0.005 meters) is used as a value tuned to work well with most situations with game-sized objects described in meters. If you decide to work in a different unit system (such as pixels) then 0.005 pixels is not a good value for this constant and would be too precise, leading to numerical problems, especially far from the origin. Instead you should determine roughly how many pixels you have per meter. For example, say you want 32 pixels per meter then you should set the lengthUnitsPerMeter to be 32.0f. Setting a value of (say) 32.05 would result in the 5mm being scaled up to 0.16 meters, which is a more reasonable value for determining if shapes are touching and hull vertices are too close.
A good rule of thumb is to pass the pixel height of your player character to this function, so if your player character is 32 pixels high, then pass 32 to this function. Then you may confidently use pixels for all the length values sent to the physics system. All length values returned from the physics system will also then naturally be in pixels because the physics system does not do any scaling internally, however, you are now responsible for creating appropriate values for gravity, density, and forces.
Get/Set the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBodyDefinition.
Get/Set the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChainDefinition.

Controls if full 64-bit layers are used based upon LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsLowLevelSettings2D.physicsLayerNames or if not, the standard 32-bit layers based upon UnityEngine.LayerMask. If a LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsLowLevelSettings2D asset is assigned then the full layers (LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsLowLevelSettings2D.physicsLayerNames) will be used if LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsLowLevelSettings2D.useFullLayers is also active. If no LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsLowLevelSettings2D asset is assigned then the global layers (See UnityEngine.LayerMask) will be used.
A 64-bit mask, effectively 64 flags. The default enumerator will iterate all the bits that are set (1).
A 64-bit mask, effectively 64 flags.
Gets an enumerable group of bits that are currently reset (0). The bits are returned in ascending bit-index order. This uses LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsMask.ResetBitIterator.
Gets an enumerable group of bits that are currently set (1). The bits are returned in ascending bit-index order. This uses LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsMask.SetBitIterator.
All 64 bits set (1) in the PhysicsMask.

The world mover arguments used by the world mover.

The filter to use for checking casts. The advantage of a separate filter to LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsQuery.WorldMoverInput.overlapFilter is that you can check for overlaps in a different way to what you can hit when moving. For instance, you may or may not want to check for other movers in they existing in the world when moving but you want to always check them for overlap initially.
Create a default world mover input.
The mover geometry to use when checking for overlaps and casting.

Solving a movement is iterative and will continue until the maximum allowed iterations has been achieve, controlled by this value. The maximum allowed iterations will not always be used and solving will cease if the iteration movement falls below the square of the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsQuery.WorldMoverInput.moveTolerance.

Solving a movement will cease if the movement falls below the square of this value. By default, this value is extremely small. Too high a value will result in solving ceasing too quickly, too small will result in all allowed LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsQuery.WorldMoverInput.maxIterations being used.
The filter to use for checking overlaps.
The position desired for the mover to achieve. This is typically calculated using the current velocity, any gravity required and time-integrated by the simulation time-step (delta-time).
The transform used to transform the geometry i.e. the mover starting pose.

The velocity used to calculate the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsQuery.WorldMoverInput.targetPosition. This is not used for movement but it will be returned, modified by any surfaces hit. This velocity can then be used in subsequent inputs for movement.
Create a default world mover input.
The world mover result used by the world mover.
The final transform the mover finished at. The transform rotation is always the same as the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsQuery.WorldMoverInput.transform provided.
The final velocity the mover finished at.
The results from performing any Overlap query.
Check if the result is valid.
The shape that was detected by the overlap.

A joint constraint used to control the relative movement two bodies while still being responsive to collisions. A spring controls the position and rotation and velocity control allows for simulated friction such as seen in top-down games. A typical usage is to control the movement of a dynamic body with respect to the ground.
The desired angular velocity.
The second body the joint constrains.
A local anchor point on the first body for the constraint.

Get/Set the System.Object that event callbacks for this joint will be sent to. Care should be taken with any System.Object assigned as a callback target that isn't a UnityEngine.Object as this assignment will not in itself keep the object alive and can be garbage collected. To avoid this, you should have at least a single reference to the object in your code.
This includes the following events:
- A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.JointThresholdEvent with call LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IJointThresholdCallback.
Whether the shapes on the pair of bodies can come into contact.
Get the current angular separation error for this joint, in degrees. This does not consider admissible movement.
Get the current constraint force used by the joint, usually in newtons.
Get the current constraint torque used by the joint, usually in newtons.
Get the current linear separation error for this joint, usually in meters. This does not consider admissible movement.
Controls the scaling of the joint drawing.
The force threshold beyond which a joint event will be produced.
Get if the joint is owned. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.SetOwner.
Checks if the joint is valid.
Gets the joint type. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.JointType.
The desired linear velocity.
The local anchor frame constraint relative to bodyA's origin.
The local anchor frame constraint relative to bodyB's origin.
The maximum linear force, usually in newtons. A value of zero is a special case which turns the limit off.
The maximum torque, usually in newton-meters. A value of zero is a special case which turns the limit off.
Get LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose, typically by the owner only.
The spring angular damping.
The spring angular frequency, in cycles per second. A value of zero is a special case which turns the angular spring off.
The spring linear damping.
The spring linear frequency, in cycles per second. A value of zero is a special case which turns the linear spring off.
The spring maximum linear force, usually in newtons. A value of zero is a special case which turns the force limit off.
The spring maximum torque, usually in newton-meters. A value of zero is a special case which turns the torque limit off.
The torque threshold beyond which a joint event will be produced.
Controls the joint stiffness damping, non-dimensional. Use 1 for critical damping.
Controls the joint stiffness frequency, in cycles per second.
Get/Set LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose. The physics system doesn't use this data, it is entirely for custom use.
Get the world the body is attached to.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsRelativeJoint.SetOwner(UnityEngine.Object)

summary.para: Set the (optional) owner object associated with this joint and return an owner key that must be specified when destroying the joint with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.Destroy. The physics system provides access to all objects, including the ability to destroy them so this feature can be used to stop accidental destruction of objects that are owned by other objects. You can only set the owner once, multiple attempts will produce a warning. The lifetime of the specified owner object is not linked to this joint i.e. this joint will still be owned by the owner object, even if it is destroyed. It is also valid to not specify an owner object (NULL) to simply gain an owner key however it can be useful, if simply for debugging purposes and discovery, to know which object is the owner.

member.param: The object that owns this joint. This can be NULL if not required.

returns.para: An owner key that must be passed to LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.Destroy when destroying the joint.

Controls the joint stiffness frequency, in cycles per second.
