summary.para:
A shape is attached to a body and defines an area to which two distinct types of behaviour are handled:
- Collision: Contacts between shapes produce a collision response on their respective bodies, assuming their body type is Dynamic. - Trigger: Contacts between shapes do not produce a collision response, only the fact that they're overlapping is reported.
An unlimited number of shapes can be attached to a single body, known as a compound body. A shape is automatically destroyed when the body it is attached to is destroyed. A shape cannot exist unattached from a body.

Get the world AABB that bounds this shape. The bounds of the shape is inflated slightly due to speculative collision detection. The inflation is smaller on Static shape types however it is not zero due to time-of-impact collision detection. If an exact AABB is required then you can retrieve that via the shape geometry. See LowLevelPhysics2D.CircleGeometry.CalculateAABB, LowLevelPhysics2D.CapsuleGeometry.CalculateAABB, LowLevelPhysics2D.PolygonGeometry.CalculateAABB and LowLevelPhysics2D.SegmentGeometry.CalculateAABB.
The body which the shape is attached to.

The bounciness (coefficient of restitution) usually in the range [0, 1]. Values higher than 1 will result in energy being added which can lead to an unstable simulation. This is assigned to the current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.surfaceMaterial.
Defines the method used when mixing the friction values of two shapes to form a contact. This is assigned to the current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.surfaceMaterial.

The priority for combining the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.bounciness properties when two shapes come into contact. If the priority of one shape is higher than the other shape then the higher priority LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.SurfaceMaterial.bouncinessCombine will be used. If the priority of both shapes are the same then simply the higher enumeration value of UnityEngine.PhysicsMaterialCombine2D from both shapes will be used. This is assigned to the current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.surfaceMaterial.

Get/Set the System.Object that callbacks for this shape will be sent to. Care should be taken with any System.Object assigned as a callback target that isn't a UnityEngine.Object as this assignment will not in itself keep the object alive and can be garbage collected. To avoid this, you should have at least a single reference to the object in your code.
This includes the following events:
- A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.ContactFilterEvent with call LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IContactFilterCallback. - A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.PreSolveEvent with call LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IPreSolveCallback. - A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.TriggerBeginEvent with call LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.ITriggerCallback. - A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.TriggerEndEvent with call LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.ITriggerCallback. - A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.ContactBeginEvent with call LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IContactCallback. - A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.ContactEndEvent with call LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IContactCallback.

Get/Set the Capsule associated with this shape. When getting the shape geometry, the shape type must match the geometry type otherwise an warning will be produced and an invalid geometry will be returned. Setting the geometry will change the type of shape represented even if the shape type was different before. Setting the geometry will also result in waking the body the shape is attached to.

Get the owning chain. The type of shape must be LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.ShapeType.ChainSegment otherwise a warning will be produced. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.isChainSegment and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChain.

Get the Chain Segment Geometry associated with this shape. When getting the shape geometry, the shape type must match the geometry type otherwise an warning will be produced and an invalid geometry will be returned.

Get/Set the Circle associated with this shape. When getting the shape geometry, the shape type must match the geometry type otherwise an warning will be produced and an invalid geometry will be returned. Setting the geometry will change the type of shape represented even if the shape type was different before. Setting the geometry will result in waking the body the shape is attached to.

Controls whether this shape produces contact events which can be retrieved after the simulation has completed. Any contact events can be used to call the assigned LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.callbackTarget. A contact event is produced if either shapes involved have their contactEvents enabled. A contact event will produce a LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IContactCallback to the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.callbackTarget for both shapes involved.
The filter used when determining what contacts this shape participates in.

Controls whether this shape produces contact filter callbacks. A contact filter callback allows direct control over whether a contact will be created between a pair of shapes. This applies to both triggers and non-triggers but only with to Dynamic bodies These are relatively expensive so disabling them can provide a significant performance benefit. A contact filter callback will call the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.callbackTarget for both shapes involved if they implement LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IContactFilterCallback.

Custom debug draw color. Any color value other than UnityEngine.Color.clear (RGBA=0) will be used to render the shape.. This value is passed back when using the PhysicsWorld debug drawing. The alpha value here is always ignored. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player. This is assigned to the current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.surfaceMaterial. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Draw.

Get/Set a shape definition by accessing all of its current properties. This is provided as convenience only and should not be used when performance is important as all the properties defined in the definition are accessed sequentially. You should try to only use the specific properties you need rather than using this feature.
The Coulomb (dry) friction coefficient, usually in the range [0, 1]. This is assigned to the current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.surfaceMaterial.
Defines the method used when mixing the friction values of two shapes to form a contact. This is assigned to the current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.surfaceMaterial.

The priority for combining the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.friction properties when two shapes come into contact. If the priority of one shape is higher than the other shape then the higher priority LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.SurfaceMaterial.frictionCombine will be used. If the priority of both shapes are the same then simply the higher enumeration value of UnityEngine.PhysicsMaterialCombine2D from both shapes will be used. This is assigned to the current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.surfaceMaterial.
Controls whether this shape produces hit events which can be retrieved after the simulation has completed.
Check if the shape is a Chain type. A Chain type is owned by a chain. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.chain and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChain.
Get if the shape is owned. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.SetOwner.
Get/Set if the shape is a trigger. Changing the state here is relatively expensive and should be avoided. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShapeDefinition.isTrigger.
Check if the shape is valid.
Get the center of the shape, in local-space.

The shape mass configuration. Normally this only used on a body where the total of all shapes is used. This allows the calculation of this specific shape in isolation. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.MassConfiguration.
The mover data for the shape mover.
Get LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose, typically by the owner only.

Get/Set the Polygon associated with this shape. When getting the shape geometry, the shape type must match the geometry type otherwise an warning will be produced and an invalid geometry will be returned. Setting the geometry will change the type of shape represented even if the shape type was different before. Setting the geometry will also result in waking the body the shape is attached to.

Controls whether this shape produces pre-solve callbacks. This only applies to Dynamic bodies and is ignored for triggers. These are relatively expensive so disabling them can provide a significant performance benefit. A pre-solve callback will call the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.callbackTarget for both shapes involved if they implement LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IPreSolveCallback.
The rolling resistance usually in the range [0, 1]. This is assigned to the current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.surfaceMaterial.

Get/Set the Segment associated with this shape. When getting the shape geometry, the shape type must match the geometry type otherwise an warning will be produced and an invalid geometry will be returned. Setting the geometry will change the type of shape represented even if the shape type was different before. Setting the geometry will also result in waking the body the shape is attached to.
The type of shape. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.ShapeType.

Should the body update its mass properties when this shape is created. Disabling this improves performance when multiple shapes are being added to the same body. The mass of a body can then be explicitly updated by calling PhysicsBody.ApplyMassFromShapes
See PhysicsShapeDefinition.startMassUpdate.

Normally shapes on Static bodies don't create contacts when they are added to the world. This overrides that behavior and causes contact creation. This significantly slows down Static body creation which can be important when there are many Static shapes. This is implicitly always true for Triggers, Dynamic bodies and Kinematic bodies.
See PhysicsShapeDefinition.startStaticContacts.

The surface material for the shape comprising of many properties such as friction, bounciness, rolling resistance etc. Setting the surface material overrides any individual settings for friction, bounciness, rolling resistance etc.
The tangent (surface) speed. This is assigned to the current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.surfaceMaterial.
Get the shape transform. This is simply the body transform. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.transform.

Controls whether this shape produces triggers events which can be retrieved after the simulation has completed. A trigger event is only produced if both shapes involved have their triggerEvents enabled. A trigger event will produce a LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.ITriggerCallback to the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.callbackTarget for both shapes involved.
Get/Set LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose. The physics system doesn't use this data, it is entirely for custom use.
Get the world the shape is attached to.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.ApplyWind(UnityEngine.Vector2,System.Single,System.Single,System.Boolean)

summary.para: Apply a wind force to the shape body using the density of air This considers the projected area of the shape in the wind direction. This also considers the relative velocity of the shape. This only has an effect if the shape body is UnityEngine.RigidbodyType2D.Dynamic. This only has an effect of shapes of type Circle, Capsule or Polygon.

member.param: The wind velocity in world-space.
The drag coefficient which is a force that opposes the relative velocity.
The lift coefficient which is a force that is perpendicular to the relative velocity.
Whether the shape body should be woken or not.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.ClosestPoint(UnityEngine.Vector2)

summary.para: Calculate the closest point on this shape to the specified point.

member.param: The point to check.

returns.para: The closest point on the shape to the specified point.

summary.para: The contact between two shapes. By convention the manifold normal points from shape A to shape B. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.GetContacts and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.GetContacts.

The unique Id of this contact. This contact is volatile and may be destroyed automatically when the world is modified or simulated therefore it should always be checked for validity with ContactId.isValid.
The contact manifold describing the contact.
One of the shapes involved in the contact.
The other shape involved in the contact.
A contact filter is used to control what contacts are created when intersecting other shapes. A contact filter contains a filter with the addition of a group index allowing overrides to the filter.
The categories this object is in. Usually you would only set one bit but multiple are allowed.
The categories this object will produce contacts with.
Get a default contact filter that contacts everything.

Collision groups allow a certain group of objects to never collide (negative) or always collide (positive). A group index of zero has no effect. A non-zero group always overrides the categorycontacts masks.
The rules for two shapes coming into contact are:
- If either shape has a group of zero then the group is ignored and the categorycontacts masks are used. - If both shapes have a non-zero but different group then the category/contacts masks are used. - If both shapes have an identical and positive group then they will always produce a contact. - If both shapes have an identical and negative group then they will never produce a contact.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.ContactFilter.#ctor

summary.para: Create a contact filter.

member.param: A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsMask defining the categories this object is in.
A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsMask defining the categories this object will produce contacts with.
The override group this filter belongs to with zero indicating no group. Groups allow a certain group of objects to never collide (negative) or always collide (positive).

summary.para: The default categories used.
The default contacts used.
Get a contact filter that is all categories and contacts everything.
The unique Id of the contact. This contact is volatile and may be destroyed automatically when the world is modified or simulated therefore it should always be checked for validity.
Get the contact.
Check if the contact is valid or not.
A contact manifold describes the contact points between colliding shapes. Speculative collision is used so some contact points may be separated, a property available per-contact.
The unit normal vector in world space, points from shape A to bodyB
The number of manifold points available, in the range [0, 2].
The manifold points, up to two are possible.
Angular impulse applied for rolling resistance (N " m " s = kg * m^2 / s).
The number of manifold points available that are speculative, in the range [0, 2].
Contains all the detail related to the geometry and dynamics of the contact. You may use the ManifoldPoint.totalNormalImpulse to determine if there was an interaction during the time step.
Location of the contact point relative to shapeA's origin in world space.
Location of the contact point relative to shapeB's origin in world space.
Uniquely identifies a contact point between two shapes. This should not be confused with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.ContactId.
The impulse along the manifold normal vector.
Relative normal velocity pre-solve. Used for hit events. If the normal impulse is zero then there was no hit. Negative means shapes are approaching.
Did this contact point exist the previous step?
Location of the contact point in world space. Subject to precision loss at large coordinates.
The separation of the contact point, negative if penetrating.
Is the contact point speculative i.e. not currently interacting?
The friction impulse.

The total normal impulse applied across sub-stepping and restitution. This includes the warm starting impulse, the sub-step delta impulse, and the restitution impulse. This can be used to identify speculative contact points that had an interaction during the simulation step.
Fixed-sized manifold point array.
Manifold Point #0.
Manifold Point #1.
The number of manifold points available that are speculative, in the range [0, 2].

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.Destroy(System.Boolean,System.Int32)

summary.para: Destroy the shape, destroying all LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.Contact the shape is involved in. If the object is owned with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.SetOwner then you must provide the owner key it returned. Failing to do so will return a warning and the shape will not be destroyed. The lifetime of the specified owner object is not linked to this shape i.e. this shape will still be owned by the owner object, even if it is destroyed. Shapes of type Chain cannot be destroyed here, they must be destroyed by their owning chain. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChain and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.MassConfiguration.

member.param: Optional flag indicating if the body mass configuration should be updated. Not doing so is faster, especially when destroying multiple shapes.
Optional owner key returned when using LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.SetOwner.

returns.para: If the shape was destroyed or not.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.DestroyBatch(System.ReadOnlySpan`1<UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape>,System.Boolean)

summary.para: Destroy a batch of shapes, destroying all Contact the shapes are involved in. Any invalid shapes will be ignored including chain segment shapes created via a LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChain (the chain must be destroyed)." Owned shapes will produce a warning and will not be destroyed (See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.SetOwner). See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.MassConfiguration.

member.param: The shapes to destroy.
Whether to update the body mass configuration. Not doing so is faster, especially when destroying multiple shapes.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.SetOwner(UnityEngine.Object)

summary.para: Set the (optional) owner object associated with this shape and return an owner key that must be specified when destroying the shape with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.Destroy. The physics system provides access to all objects, including the ability to destroy them so this feature can be used to stop accidental destruction of objects that are owned by other objects. You can only set the owner once, multiple attempts will produce a warning. It is also valid to not specify an owner object (NULL) to simply gain an owner key however it can be useful, if simply for debugging purposes and discovery, to know which object is the owner.

member.param: The object that owns this shape. This can be NULL if not required.

returns.para: An owner key that must be passed to LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.Destroy when destroying the shape.

summary.para: Accessor for the shape array.

member.param: The index to access.

summary.para:
A proxy of a shape in a generic form suited to representing all support shape types. You can provide between 1 and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsConstants.MaxPolygonVerticesand a radius.
- A LowLevelPhysics2D.CircleGeometry is a single point with a non-zero radius (zero radius is allowed however and defines a point). - A LowLevelPhysics2D.CapsuleGeometry is two points with a non-zero radius. - A LowLevelPhysics2D.PolygonGeometry box is the points with and an optional radius. - A LowLevelPhysics2D.SegmentGeometry is two points with a zero radius. - A LowLevelPhysics2D.ChainSegmentGeometry is two points with a zero radius.
To create a proxy, simply provide the geometry to the constructor.
Get a LowLevelPhysics2D.CapsuleGeometry from the shape proxy. The LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.ShapeProxy.count must be 2.
Get a LowLevelPhysics2D.CircleGeometry from the shape proxy. The LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.ShapeProxy.count must be 1.
The number of vertices.
Get a LowLevelPhysics2D.PolygonGeometry from the shape proxy.
The radius around the vertices.
Get a LowLevelPhysics2D.SegmentGeometry from the shape proxy. The LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.ShapeProxy.count must be 2.
The shape vertices.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.ShapeProxy.#ctor(UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.ChainSegmentGeometry)

summary.para: Create a shape proxy using the specified ChainSegment.

member.param: The geometry to use.

summary.para: The type of shape. Some shapes are "closed" meaning they have an interior which will produce contacts. Some shapes are "open" meaning they do not have an interior and will only produce contacts when their boundary is intersected.
A capsule is an extruded circle. This is a closed shape. See LowLevelPhysics2D.CapsuleGeometry.

A Chain of line segments that are joined together with other line segments. This is an open shape. This is indirectly created and owned by a chain. See LowLevelPhysics2D.ChainSegmentGeometry and LowLevelPhysics2D.ChainGeometry.
A circle with an offset. This is a closed shape. See LowLevelPhysics2D.CircleGeometry.
A convex polygon. This is a closed shape. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PolygonGeometry.
A line segment. This is an open shape. See LowLevelPhysics2D.SegmentGeometry.
Defines the dynamics of a surface on a shape.
The bounciness (coefficient of restitution) usually in the range [0, 1].
Defines the method used when mixing the bounciness values of two shapes to form a contact.

The priority for mixing the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.bounciness properties when two shapes come into contact. If the priority of one shape is higher than the other shape then the higher priority LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.SurfaceMaterial.bouncinessMixing will be used. If the priority of both shapes are the same then simply the higher enumeration value of LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.SurfaceMaterial.MixingMode from both shapes will be used.

Custom debug draw color. Any color value other than (0,0,0,0) will be used to render the shape. This value is passed back when using the PhysicsWorld debug drawing. The alpha value here is always ignored. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player. See UnityEngine.Color.clear and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Draw.
The Coulomb (dry) friction coefficient, usually in the range [0, 1].
Defines the method used when mixing the friction values of two shapes to form a contact.

The priority for mixing the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.friction properties when two shapes come into contact. If the priority of one shape is higher than the other shape then the higher priority LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.SurfaceMaterial.frictionMixing will be used. If the priority of both shapes are the same then simply the higher enumeration value of LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.SurfaceMaterial.MixingMode from both shapes will be used.
The rolling resistance usually in the range [0, 1].
The tangent (surface) speed.
Create a default surface material.
Get the default surface material.
The method used to mix friction or bounciness values.
The average of both surface values.
The maximum of both surface values.
The geometric mean of both surface values.
The minium of both surface values.
The product of both surface values.
A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape definition used to specify important initial properties.

Controls whether this shape produces contact events which can be retrieved after the simulation has completed. This only applies to kinematic and dynamic bodies. A contact event is produced if either shapes involved have their contactEvents enabled. Changing this at run-time may lead to lost begin/end events.
The contact filter used to control which contacts this shape can participate in.

Controls whether this shape produces contact filter callbacks. A contact filter callback allows direct control over whether a contact will be created between a pair of shapes. This applies to both triggers and non-triggers but only with to Dynamic bodies These are relatively expensive so disabling them can provide a significant performance benefit. A contact filter callback will call the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.callbackTarget for both shapes involved if they implement LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IContactFilterCallback.
Get a default LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape definition.
The density, usually in kg/m^2, defaults to 1. This is not part of the surface material because this is for the interior of the shape, which may have other considerations, such as being hollow.
Controls whether this shape produces hit events which can be retrieved after the simulation has completed. This only applies to kinematic and dynamic bodies. This is ignored for triggers.

Enable/Disable being a trigger shape. A trigger shape generates overlap events but never generates a collision response. Triggers do not collide with other triggers and do not have continuous collision, instead, use a ray or shape cast for those scenarios. Triggers still contribute to the body mass if they have non-zero density. The default is false.
The mover data used for the shape mover.

Controls whether this shape produces pre-solve callbacks. This only applies to Dynamic bodies and is ignored for triggers. These are relatively expensive so disabling them can provide a significant performance benefit. A pre-solve callback will call the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.callbackTarget for both shapes involved if they implement LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IPreSolveCallback.

Should the body update its mass properties when this shape is created. Disabling this improves performance when multiple shapes are being added to the same body. The mass of a body can then be explicitly updated by calling PhysicsBody.ApplyMassFromShapes
See PhysicsShape.startMassUpdate.

Normally shapes on Static bodies don't create contacts when they are added to the world. This overrides that behavior and causes contact creation. This significantly slows down Static body creation which can be important when there are many Static shapes. This is implicitly always true for Triggers, Dynamic bodies and Kinematic bodies.
See PhysicsShape.startStaticContacts.
The surface material for the shape comprising of many properties such as friction, bounciness, rolling resistance etc.

Controls whether this shape produces trigger events which can be retrieved after the simulation has completed. A trigger event is only produced if both shapes involved have their triggerEvents enabled. This applies to triggers and non-triggers alike.
Create a default LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape definition.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShapeDefinition.#ctor(System.Boolean)

summary.para: Create a default LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape definition.

member.param: Controls whether the default come settings from the physics settings or not.

summary.para: A joint that requires defining a line of motion defined by the local anchor A. Body B may slide along the axis defined by the local anchor A. Body B cannot rotate relative to body A. The joint translation is zero when the local anchor origins coincide in world space. The joint uses local anchors so that the initial configuration can violate the constraint slightly.
The second body the joint constrains.
A local anchor point on the first body for the constraint.

Get/Set the System.Object that event callbacks for this joint will be sent to. Care should be taken with any System.Object assigned as a callback target that isn't a UnityEngine.Object as this assignment will not in itself keep the object alive and can be garbage collected. To avoid this, you should have at least a single reference to the object in your code.
This includes the following events:
- A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.JointThresholdEvent with call LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IJointThresholdCallback.
Whether the shapes on the pair of bodies can come into contact.
Get the current angular separation error for this joint, in degrees. This does not consider admissible movement.
Get the current constraint force used by the joint, usually in newtons.
Get the current constraint torque used by the joint, usually in newtons.
Get the current linear separation error for this joint, usually in meters. This does not consider admissible movement.
Get the current motor force, usually in newtons.
Get the current joint translation speed, usually in meters per second.
Get the current joint translation, usually in meters.
Controls the scaling of the joint drawing.
Enable/Disable the joint translation limit.
Enable/Disable the joint motor.
Enable/Disable a spring along the slider joint axis.
The force threshold beyond which a joint event will be produced.
Get if the joint is owned. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.SetOwner.
Checks if the joint is valid.
Gets the joint type. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.JointType.
The local anchor frame constraint relative to bodyA's origin.
The local anchor frame constraint relative to bodyB's origin.
Get the lower translation limit.
The maximum force the motor can apply, usually in newtons.
The desired motor speed, usually in meters per second.
Get LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose, typically by the owner only.
The spring damping, non-dimensional.
The spring stiffness, in cycles per second.
The spring target translation, usually in meters. The spring-damper will drive to this translation.
The torque threshold beyond which a joint event will be produced.
Controls the joint stiffness damping, non-dimensional. Use 1 for critical damping.
Controls the joint stiffness frequency, in cycles per second.
Get the upper translation limit.
Get/Set LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose. The physics system doesn't use this data, it is entirely for custom use.
Get the world the body is attached to.

Get the current constraint torque used by the joint, usually in newtons.
Get the current linear separation error for this joint, usually in meters. This does not consider admissible movement.
Get the current motor torque, usually in newtons.
Controls the scaling of the joint drawing.
Enable/disable the joint limit.
Enable/Disable the joint motor.
Enable/Disable a spring along the joint axis.
The force threshold beyond which a joint event will be produced.
Get if the joint is owned. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.SetOwner.
Checks if the joint is valid.
Gets the joint type. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.JointType.
The local anchor frame constraint relative to bodyA's origin.
The local anchor frame constraint relative to bodyB's origin.
Get/Set the lower translation limit.
The maximum torque the motor can apply, usually in newtons.
The desired motor speed, usually in degrees per second.
Get LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose, typically by the owner only.
The spring damping, non-dimensional.
The spring stiffness, in cycles per second.
The torque threshold beyond which a joint event will be produced.
Controls the joint stiffness damping, non-dimensional. Use 1 for critical damping.
Controls the joint stiffness frequency, in cycles per second.
Get/Set the upper translation limit.
Get/Set LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose. The physics system doesn't use this data, it is entirely for custom use.
Get the world the body is attached to.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWheelJoint.SetOwner(UnityEngine.Object)

summary.para: Set the (optional) owner object associated with this joint and return an owner key that must be specified when destroying the joint with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.Destroy. The physics system provides access to all objects, including the ability to destroy them so this feature can be used to stop accidental destruction of objects that are owned by other objects. You can only set the owner once, multiple attempts will produce a warning. The lifetime of the specified owner object is not linked to this joint i.e. this joint will still be owned by the owner object, even if it is destroyed. It is also valid to not specify an owner object (NULL) to simply gain an owner key however it can be useful, if simply for debugging purposes and discovery, to know which object is the owner.

member.param: The object that owns this joint. This can be NULL if not required.

returns.para: An owner key that must be passed to LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.Destroy when destroying the joint.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWheelJointDefinition.#ctor(System.Boolean)

summary.para: Create a default LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWheelJoint ] definition.

member.param: Controls whether the default settings come from the physics settings or not.

summary.para: A world is a container for all other physics objects such as LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody, LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape, LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint etc. A world can be simulated in isolation from all other worlds. The maximum number of worlds that can be created at one time is defined by LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsConstants.MaxWorlds. A world is completely isolated from all other worlds.

The distance used to expand AABBs in the broadphase dynamic tree, in meters. This allows broadphase proxies to move by a small amount without triggering a tree adjustment. This value is 0.05f * LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.lengthUnitsPerMeter. Normally this is 5cm.
Controls if body update callback targets are automatically called. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SendBodyUpdateCallbacks
Controls if shape contact callback targets are automatically called. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SendContactCallbacks
Controls if joint threshold callback targets are automatically called. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SendJointThresholdCallbacks
Controls if shape trigger callback targets are automatically called. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SendTriggerCallbacks
Get the number of awake bodies in the world.

The maximum rotation of a body per time step, in degrees. This limit is very large and is used to prevent numerical problems. This value is approximately 45-degrees or 0.25f * LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsMath.PI radians.
The time that a body must be still before it will go to sleep, in seconds. This value is 0.5f

Get the body events from the last simulation. The LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody objects returned should be checked to see if they are valid before accessing as they may have been deleted since this event was produced (see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.isValid). Any change to the world state can invalidate this data so referring to this data afterwards may cause an unavoidable crash! It is recommended that you immediately extract what information you need and not directly reference the returned data. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.BodyUpdateEvent.
Adjust the bounce threshold, usually in meters per second. It is recommended not to make this value very small because it will prevent bodies from sleeping.

Get if the low-level physics is currently bypassed or not. When bypassed, no simulations or rendering will occur reducing it to near-zero CPU overhead. This can be controlled via LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsLowLevelSettings2D.bypassLowLevel.

Controls how many simulations can be started in parallel. Whilst running simulations in parallel can improver overall performance, workers should ideally be left free for the simulation solver otherwise it may degrade solving performance. The actual quantity of workers used will always be capped to those available on the current device. If the total number of workers available is below 4 then parallel simulation won't occur as generally this would reduce overall performance, however parallel solving of each simulation using workers will still be used. This should not be confused with the quantity of workers used when solving a simulation.

Get the contact begin events from the last simulation. The LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape objects returned should be checked to see if they are valid before accessing as they may have been deleted since this event was produced (see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.isValid). The LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.Contact objects returned should be checked to see if they are valid before accessing as they may have been deleted since this event was produced (see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.Contact.isValid). Any change to the world state can invalidate this data so referring to this data afterwards may cause an unavoidable crash! It is recommended that you immediately extract what information you need and not directly reference the returned data. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.ContactBeginEvent.
The contact bounciness with 1 being critical damping (non-dimensional).

Get the contact end events from the last simulation. The LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape objects returned should be checked to see if they are valid before accessing as they may have been deleted since this event was produced (see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.isValid). The LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.Contact objects returned should be checked to see if they are valid before accessing as they may have been deleted since this event was produced (see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.Contact.isValid). Any change to the world state can invalidate this data so referring to this data afterwards may cause an unavoidable crash! It is recommended that you immediately extract what information you need and not directly reference the returned data. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.ContactEndEvent.

Controls if contact filter callbacks will be called. A contact filter callback allows direct control over whether a contact will be created between a pair of shapes. This applies to both triggers and non-triggers but only with Dynamic bodies. These are relatively expensive so disabling them can provide a significant performance benefit. A contact filter callback will call the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.callbackTarget for both shapes involved if they implement LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IContactFilterCallback.
The contact stiffness, in cycles per second.

Get the contact hit events from the last simulation. The LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape objects returned should be checked to see if they are valid before accessing as they may have been deleted since this event was produced (see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.isValid). Any change to the world state can invalidate this data so referring to this data afterwards may cause an unavoidable crash! It is recommended that you immediately extract what information you need and not directly reference the returned data. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.ContactHitEvent.
The contact hit event threshold controls the collision speed needed to generate a contact hit event, usually in meters per second. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.ContactHitEvent.
The contact speed used to solve overlaps, in meters per second.

Controls if continuous collision detection will be used between Dynamic and Static bodies. Generally you should keep continuous collision enabled to prevent fast moving objects from going through Static objects. The performance gain from disabling continuous collision is minor.
Get the world counters.

Get the default world created at start-up. This world cannot be destroyed as it is permanently owned by Unity itself. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SetOwner and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.isOwned.

Get/Set a world definition by accessing all of its current properties. This is provided as convenience only and should not be used when performance is important as all the properties defined in the definition are accessed sequentially. You should try to only use the specific properties you need rather than using this feature.

Controls the draw capacity. The draw capacity of the buffers when drawing are initially zero however increasing this value will mean buffers won't be resized when more elements are drawn and therefore no GC allocations will occur. Changes won't take effect until exiting play mode. This value directly controls the capacity for each element type drawn. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player.

Controls what colors are used to draw LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody, LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape, LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint etc. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player.

Controls the draw fill alpha. This is used to scale the interior fill alpha and is only used when LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.DrawFillOptions.Outline is used so that the interior color can be distinguished from the outline color by transparency. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player.
Controls what aspects of is drawn using LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Draw. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player.
Controls the joint contact impulse scale used when drawing contact impulses. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player.
Controls the joint contact normal scale used when drawing contact normals. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player.
Draw Options used to control what is drawn using LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Draw. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.DrawOptions.
Controls the draw point scale used when drawing points. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player.
Controls the draw thickness (outline and orientation). This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player.

Controls the element depth.
When using custom drawing of geometry or primitive shapes there is no reference to the orthogonal axis used with respect to the current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.transformPlane.
The element depth is in world-space and for each transform plan is defined as:
- Element depth is rendered along the Z axis when using LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.TransformPlane.XY. - Element depth is rendered along the Y axis when using LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.TransformPlane.XZ. - Element depth is rendered along the X axis when using LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.TransformPlane.ZY.
You should set the element depth before performing any custom draw.
The element depth will be reset to zero when rendering is complete.
This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player.
Get the world counters, summed for all the active worlds.
Get the world timing profile, summed for all the active worlds.
GetSet the gravity vector applied to all bodies in the world, usually in ms^2.

Positions greater than about 16km will have precision problems, so 100km as a limit should be fine in all cases. This is used to detect bad values. This value is 100000.0f * LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.lengthUnitsPerMeter.
Check if this is the default LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld. The default world is automatically created at start-up.
Check if the world is empty as defined by having no bodies, shapes or joints.
Get if the world is owned. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SetOwner.

Get if rendering is currently allowed. Rendering is always allowed in the Editor however it is only allowed in build if LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsLowLevelSettings2D.drawInBuild is active when building.
Check if the world is valid.

Get the joint events from the last simulation. An event is produced by a Joint which exceeds either its LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.forceThreshold or LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.torqueThreshold. The LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint objects returned should be checked to see if they are valid before accessing as they may have been deleted since this event was produced (see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.isValid). Any change to the world state can invalidate this data so referring to this data afterwards may cause an unavoidable crash! It is recommended that you immediately extract what information you need and not directly reference the returned data. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.JointThresholdEvent.
Get the delta-time used for the last simulation run.
Get the timestamp when the last simulation was run.

Get the internal length units per meter. Changes won't take effect until exiting play mode. The physics system bases all length units on meters but you may need different units for your project. You can set this value to use different units but it should only be modified before any other calls to the physics system occur and only modified once. Changing this value after any physics object has been created can result in severe simulation instabilities.
For example, if your game uses pixels for units you can use pixels for all length values sent to the physics system. There should be no extra cost however, the physics system has some internal tolerances and thresholds that have been tuned for meters. By calling this function, the physics system is better able to adjust those tolerances and thresholds to improve accuracy. A good rule of thumb is to pass the height of your player character to this function. So if your player character is 32 pixels high, then pass 32 to this function. Then you may confidently use pixels for all the length values sent to the physics system. All length values returned from the physics system will also then be in pixels because the physics system does not do any scaling internally, however, you are now on the hook for coming up with good values for gravity, density, and forces.
The default value is 1.

A small length used as a collision and constraint tolerance, in meters. Usually it is chosen to be numerically significant, but visually insignificant. This value is 0.005f * LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.lengthUnitsPerMeter. Normally this is 0.5cm.
Get/Set the maximum linear speed.
Get LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose, typically by the owner only.
Get/Set if the world is paused. When paused, any simulation attempted will be ignored whether it be automatic or manual.

Controls if pre-solve callbacks will be called. This only applies to Dynamic bodies and is ignored for triggers. These are relatively expensive so disabling them can provide a significant performance benefit. A pre-solve callback will call the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.callbackTarget for both shapes involved if they implement LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IPreSolveCallback.
Get the world timing profile.

Get/Set whether safety threading locks are enabled or not. Locks are enabled by default however on platforms that do not support threading, locks are not used. Disabling locks can result in a small performance boost however, please note the following EXTREME CAUTIONS.
Typically, per-world, multiple read operations can happen in parallel however only a single write operation can occur concurrently.
Read and write operations can never happen at the same time. Locking is a self-balancing reader-preferred system that tries to reduce writers "starving". Once a writer is in a queue, it registers incoming readers as waiting readers and, once active readers are handled, it starts processing a single writer. After that writer has been handled, it flips waiting readers into active readers and processes them. Whilst this system is extremely fast, it does have a very small overhead. Disabling this system can give a small performance boost but is nearly always not worth it therefore this option should be used for testing only.
EXTREME CAUTION should be taken if disabling locks on platforms that support threading! A majority of this API is thread-safe and is is due to the safety locks! Locks are used to ensure that read and write operations do not interfere with each other. Locks also ensure that no read or write operations happen during a simulation step. Overlapping read or write operations will almost certainly result in corruptions and a subsequent crash, so unless you are absolutely sure this is not the case, do not disable locks!
Get/Set the simulation sub-steps to use during simulation. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Simulate.
Get/Set the simulation type which controls when or if the simulation will be automatically simulated. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SimulationType.

Get/Set the simulation worker count for the world. The actual quantity of workers used will always be capped to those available on the current device and reading the property will return the number of workers actually being used by the device. Changing the worker count continuously is not recommend and will impact performance as it requires the task queue be recreated. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorldDefinition.simulationWorkers.
Controls if bodies go to sleep when not moving and not interacting. Sleeping can provide a significant performance improvement when many Dynamic or Kinematic bodies are in the world.
The distance at which speculative contacts will be calculated. This reduces jitter. This value is 4.0f * LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.lengthUnitsPerMeter. Normally this is 2cm.
Controls the transform plane that the world uses when writing transforms. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.transformWriteMode.

Controls if Transform tweening is used. Transform tweening is where bodies that have their LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.transformObject set, write to the UnityEngine.Transform each frame depending on the specific body LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.TransformWriteMode set. Regardless of this setting, Transform tweening is never used if the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.simulationType is LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SimulationType.Update or LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.transformWriteMode is LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.TransformWriteMode.Off.

Controls how transform writing is handled. Only bodies that have their LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.transformWriteMode active and produce a LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.BodyUpdateEvent will write to a transform. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.TransformWriteMode.

Get the trigger begin events from the last simulation. The LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape objects returned should be checked to see if they are valid before accessing as they may have been deleted since this event was produced (see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.isValid). Any change to the world state can invalidate this data so referring to this data afterwards may cause an unavoidable crash! It is recommended that you immediately extract what information you need and not directly reference the returned data. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.TriggerBeginEvent.

Get the trigger end events from the last simulation. The LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape objects returned should be checked to see if they are valid before accessing as they may have been deleted since this event was produced (see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.isValid). Any change to the world state can invalidate this data so referring to this data afterwards may cause an unavoidable crash! It is recommended that you immediately extract what information you need and not directly reference the returned data. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.TriggerEndEvent.

Indicates if the option of LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsLowLevelSettings2D.useFullLayers is active or not. If no LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsLowLevelSettings2D asset is assigned, this option will return false (inactive). When active, the 64-bit layers are used (see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsLowLevelSettings2D.physicsLayerNames) for property drawers and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsLayers.GetLayerMask. When inactive, the 32-bit layers are used (see UnityEngine.LayerMask) for property drawers and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsLayers.GetLayerMask. In all cases, the physics system itself will always use the full 64-bit layers assigned, however when using 32-bit layers, the top 32-bits will be set to zero.

Get/Set LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose. This cannot be set on the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.defaultWorld and will always be at the default. The physics system doesn't use this data, it is entirely for custom use.
Is warm-starting allowed in the world? Disabling warming-starting will severely impact stability. This is typically used for testing only!
Get the number of created worlds. This will be a value in the range of 1 to LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsConstants.MaxWorlds.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.CastGeometry(UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.CircleGeometry,UnityEngine.Vector2,UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsQuery/QueryFilter,UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsQuery/WorldCastMode,Unity.Collections.Allocator)

summary.para: Returns the shape(s) that intersect the specified Circle geometry as it is cast through the world. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsQuery.QueryFilter, LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsQuery.WorldCastMode, LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsQuery.WorldCastResult and Unity.Collections.Allocator

member.param: The Circle geometry used to cast through the world. This must be in world-space.
The translation relative to the geometry defining the direction the geometry will move through the world.
The filter to control what results are returned.
Controls how many and in what order the results are returned.
The memory allocator to use for the results. This can only be Unity.Collections.Allocator.Temp, Unity.Collections.Allocator.TempJob or Unity.Collections.Allocator.Persistent.

returns.para: The query cast results. This NativeArray must be disposed of after use otherwise leaks will occur. The exception to this is if the array is empty.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Destroy(System.Int32)

summary.para: Destroy a world, destroying all objects contained within it such as all LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody and attached LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint. If the object is owned with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SetOwner then you must provide the owner key it returned. Failing to do so will return a warning and the world will not be destroyed. You cannot destroy the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.defaultWorld as it is permanently owned by Unity itself.

member.param: Optional owner key returned when using LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SetOwner.

returns.para: If the world was destroyed or not.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.DestroyBodyBatch(System.ReadOnlySpan`1<UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody>)

summary.para: Destroy a batch of bodies. Any invalid bodies will be ignored. Owned bodies will produce a warning and will not be destroyed (See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.SetOwner).

member.param: The bodies to destroy.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.DestroyJointBatch(System.ReadOnlySpan`1<UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint>)

summary.para: Destroy a batch of joints. Any invalid joints will be ignored. Owned joints will produce a warning and will not be destroyed (LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.SetOwner).

member.param: The joints to destroy.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.DestroyShapeBatch(System.ReadOnlySpan`1<UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape>,System.Boolean)

summary.para: Destroy a batch of shapes, destroying all LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.Contact the shapes are involved in. Any invalid shapes will be ignored including chain segment shapes created via a LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsChain (the chain must be destroyed)." Owned shapes will produce a warning and will not be destroyed (LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.SetOwner). See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.MassConfiguration.

member.param: The shapes to destroy.
Whether to update the body mass configuration. Not doing so is faster, especially when destroying multiple shapes.

summary.para: The colors used to draw LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody, LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape, LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint etc.
A shape that is attached to an awake body.
A shape that is attached to a dynamic body with zero mass.
A shape that is attached to a disabled body.
A shape that is attached to a body that is awake and has fast collisions allowed.
A shape that is attached to a body with a Kinematic body type.
A shape that is attached to a body that is currently moving fast.
A shape that is attached to a body that is currently having its speed capped.
A shape that is attached to a body with a Static body type.
A shape that is attached to a body that had a time-of-impact event.
A contact that was added during the last simulation step.
The contact friction being applied.
The contact impulse being applied.
A contact normal.
A contact that already existed at the start of the last simulation step.
A contact that is speculative.
The shape bounds.
The default color used when no other shape state is indicated.
A shape that is marked as a trigger.
A solver island region.
The X component of the Transform axis.
The Y component of the Transform axis.
Controls what aspects of is drawn using LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Draw.

A combination drawn of:
- LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.DrawFillOptions.Interior - LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.DrawFillOptions.Outline - LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.DrawFillOptions.Orientation
The interior of the area is drawn.
The orientation of the area is drawn (if applicable). This is only drawn if the Outline is drawn.
The outline of the area is drawn.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Explode(UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld/ExplosionDefinition)

summary.para: Apply a radial explosion applying impulses away from the position to all bodies found within in the radius.

member.param: The explosion definition describing how the explosion should be handled.

summary.para: Used to define the parameters when using LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Explode.
Create a default explode definition.
The falloff distance beyond the radius. Impulse is reduced to zero at this distance.
The categories that will produce hits.

Impulse per unit length. This applies an impulse according to the shape perimeter that is facing the explosion. Explosions only apply to circles, capsules, and polygons. This may be negative for implosions.
The center of the explosion in world space.
The radius of the explosion.
Create a default explode definition.

summary.para: Returns all shapes that potentially overlap the provided AABB. The overlap is between AABB of shapes in the world therefore it may not result in an exact overlap of the shape itself. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsAABB, LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsQuery.QueryFilter, LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsQuery.WorldOverlapResult and Unity.Collections.Allocator.

member.param: The AABB used to check overlap. This must be in world-space.
The filter to control what results are returned.
The memory allocator to use for the results. This can only be Unity.Collections.Allocator.Temp, Unity.Collections.Allocator.TempJob or Unity.Collections.Allocator.Persistent.

returns.para: The query overlap results. This NativeArray must be disposed of after use otherwise leaks will occur. The exception to this is if the array is empty.

summary.para: Reset the world to a canonical state so that it will reproduce identical results each time. The world must be empty for this to be called otherwise a warning is produced.

Send all callbacks to targets:
- LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SendBodyUpdateCallbacks - LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SendTriggerCallbacks - LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SendContactCallbacks - LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SendJointThresholdCallbacks

Send all current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.bodyUpdateEvents where the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody involved are valid (see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.isValid) and have a callback target assigned (see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.callbackTarget). Only callback targets that implement LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IBodyUpdateCallback will be called. This will be called automatically if LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.autoBodyUpdateCallbacks is true. This must be called on the main thread.

Send all current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.contactBeginEvents and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.contactEndEvents where either of the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape involved are valid (see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.isValid) and have a callback target assigned (see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.callbackTarget). These events will only be created if both of the shape pairs has LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.contactEvents set to true. Only callback targets that implement LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IContactCallback will be called. This will be called automatically if LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.autoContactCallbacks is true. This must be called on the main thread.

Send all current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.jointThresholdEvents where the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint involved are valid (see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.isValid) and have a callback target assigned (see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.callbackTarget). These events will only be created if the joint exceeds its LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.forceThreshold or LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint.torqueThreshold. Only callback targets that implement LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IJointThresholdCallback will be called. This will be called automatically if LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.autoJointThresholdCallbacks is true. This must be called on the main thread.

Send all current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.triggerBeginEvents and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.triggerEndEvents where either of the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape involved are valid (see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.isValid) and have a callback target assigned (see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.callbackTarget). These events will only be created if one of the shape pairs has LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.triggerEvents set to true. Only callback targets that implement LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.ITriggerCallback will be called. This will be called automatically if LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.autoTriggerCallbacks is true. This must be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SetElementDepth3D(UnityEngine.Vector3)

summary.para:
Set the element depth using the specified 3D position. The relevant axis will be extracted using the current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.transformPlane. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player.
For more details, see LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.elementDepth.

member.param: The 3D position to extract the element depth from.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SetOwner(UnityEngine.Object)

summary.para: Set the (optional) owner object associated with this world and return an owner key that must be specified when destroying the world with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Destroy. The physics system provides access to all objects, including the ability to destroy them so this feature can be used to stop accidental destruction of objects that are owned by other objects. You can only set the owner once, multiple attempts will produce a warning. The lifetime of the specified owner object is not linked to this world i.e. this world will still be owned by the owner object, even if it is destroyed. It is also valid to not specify an owner object (NULL) to simply gain an owner key however it can be useful, if simply for debugging purposes and discovery, to know which object is the owner.

member.param: The object that owns this world. This can be NULL if not required.

returns.para: An owner key that must be passed to LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Destroy when destroying the body.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SetOwnerUserData(UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData,System.Int32)

summary.para: Set LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsUserData that can be used for any purpose, typically by the owner only.

member.param: The user data to set.
Optional owner key returned when using LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SetOwner.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Simulate(System.Single)

summary.para: Simulate the world. The world should must have its LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.simulationType set to LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SimulationType.Script for this to work.

member.param: The amount of time to forward simulate the world.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Simulate(System.ReadOnlySpan`1<UnityEngine.LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld>,System.Single)

summary.para: Simulate a batch of worlds. The worlds can be simulated concurrently depending on the setting of PhysicsLowLevelSettings2D.concurrentSimulations. The worlds in the batch must have their LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.simulationType set to LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SimulationType.Script.

member.param: The worlds to forward simulate.
The amount of time to forward simulate the world.

summary.para: Defines when the simulation will run.
The simulation will automatically run during the FixedUpdate.
The simulation will only run when manually called with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Simulate.
The simulation will automatically run during the Update.

summary.para: Defines the 2D Transform plane where Transform writes will occur. This also defines the rotation axis which will automatically be perpendicular to the selected plane. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.transformPlane.
XY plane with anti-clockwise Z rotation.
XZ plane with anti-clockwise Y rotation.
ZY plane with anti-clockwise X rotation.
Defines how the 2D Transforms from each LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody are written to the 3D Transform system.

Transforms are written but the rotation is converted to a UnityEngine.Quaternion where only a single axis is written, all others will be set to zero rotation. This is the fastest method of writing transforms however, any 3D rotations or rotations on the unused axis will be reset to zero. The rotational axis written to depends on the current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.TransformPlane selected with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.transformPlane where it will always be perpendicular to the transform plane.
Transforms are never written. This is the fastest operation.

Transforms are written but the rotation is converted to a UnityEngine.Quaternion where the rotation of the body transform is merged into the existing 3D rotation. This is the slowest method of writing transforms however, all 3D rotations are preserved. The rotational axis written to depends on the current LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.TransformPlane selected with LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.transformPlane where it will always be perpendicular to the transform plane.
PhysicsWorld counters that give details of the world simulation size.
The number of all body types.
The broadphase tree height for both Dynamic and Kinematic bodies.
The number of contacts.
The number of islands.
The number of joints.
The total byte allocation used by the physics system.
The number of shapes.
The number of bytes assigned to the Stack allocator.
The broadphase tree height for Static bodies.
The number of multi-threaded tasks requested solving the simulation.

summary.para: PhysicsWorld profile that contains the timings of specific world simulation stages. All times are in milliseconds.
Time spent applying bounciness.
Time spent updating body transforms.
Time spent refitting the broadphase.
Time spent updating collision pairs and creating contacts.
Time spent updating contacts.
Time spent calculate fast triggers for bodies.
Time spent generating contact hit events.
Time spent integrating transforms.
Time spent integrating velocities.
Time spent generating joint threshold events.
Time spent preparing joint and contact constraints.
Time spent preparing simulation stages.
Time spent relaxing constraint impulses.
Time spent stepping the simulation forward.
Time spent updating islands that need to sleep.
Time spent solving constraints.
Time spent solving continuous collision detection.
Time spent solving impulses.
Time spent integrating velocities, solving velocity constraints, and integrating positions.
Time spent splitting islands because some contacts and/or joints have been removed.
Time spent storing impulses.
Time spent updating triggers.
Time spent warm-starting.
Time spent writing the body poses to the transform system.

summary.para: A LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld definition used to specify important initial properties.
Controls if body update callback targets are automatically called. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SendBodyUpdateCallbacks.
Controls if shape contact callback targets are automatically called. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SendContactCallbacks.
Controls if joint threshold callback targets are automatically called. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SendJointThresholdCallbacks.
Controls if shape trigger callback targets are automatically called. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SendTriggerCallbacks.

Adjust the bounce threshold, usually in meters per second. It is recommended not to make this value very small because it will prevent bodies from sleeping. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.bounceThreshold.
The contact bounciness with 1 being critical damping (non-dimensional). See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.contactDamping.

Controls if contact filter callbacks will be called. A contact filter callback allows direct control over whether a contact will be created between a pair of shapes. This applies to both triggers and non-triggers but only with Dynamic bodies. These are relatively expensive so disabling them can provide a significant performance benefit. A contact filter callback will call the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.callbackTarget for both shapes involved if they implement LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IContactFilterCallback.
The contact stiffness, in cycles per second. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.contactFrequency.

The contact hit event threshold controls the collision speed needed to generate a contact hit event, usually in meters per second. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.ContactHitEvent. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.contactHitEventThreshold.
The contact speed used to solve overlaps, in meters per second. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.contactSpeed.

Controls if continuous collision detection will be used between Dynamic and Static bodies. Generally you should keep continuous collision enabled to prevent fast moving objects from going through Static objects. The performance gain from disabling continuous collision is minor. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.continuousAllowed
Get a default LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld definition.

Controls the draw capacity. The draw capacity of the buffers when drawing are initially zero however increasing this value will mean buffers won't be resized when more elements are drawn and therefore no GC allocations will occur. Changes won't take effect until exiting play mode. This value directly controls the capacity for each element type drawn. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.drawCapacity.

Controls what colors are used to draw LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody, LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape, LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsJoint etc. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.DrawColors.

Controls the draw fill alpha. This is used to scale the interior fill alpha and is only used when LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.DrawFillOptions.Outline is used so that the interior color can be distinguished from the outline color by transparency. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.drawFillAlpha.
Controls what aspects of is drawn using LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Draw. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.DrawFillOptions.
Controls the joint contact impulse scale used when drawing contact impulses. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.drawImpulseScale.
Controls the joint contact normal scale used when drawing contact normals. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.drawNormalScale.
Draw Options used to control what is drawn using LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Draw. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.DrawOptions.
Controls the draw point scale used when drawing points. This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.drawPointScale.
Controls the draw thickness (outline and orientation). This is only used in the Unity Editor or in a Development Player. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.drawThickness.

Get/Set the simulation worker count for the world. The actual quantity of workers used will always be capped to those available on the current device and reading the property will return the number of workers actually being used by the device. Changing the worker count continuously is not recommend and will impact performance as it requires the task queue be recreated. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.gravity.
Get/Set the maximum linear speed. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.maximumLinearSpeed.

Controls if pre-solve callbacks will be called. This only applies to Dynamic bodies and is ignored for triggers. These are relatively expensive so disabling them can provide a significant performance benefit. A pre-solve callback will call the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsShape.callbackTarget for both shapes involved if they implement LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsCallbacks.IPreSolveCallback.
Get/Set the simulation mode which controls when or if the simulation will be automatically simulated. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SimulationType and LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Simulate.
Get/Set the simulation sub-steps to use during simulation. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.Simulate. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.simulationSubSteps.

Get/Set the simulation worker count for the world. The actual quantity of workers used will always be capped to those available on the current device and reading the property will return the number of workers actually being used by the device. Changing the worker count continuously is not recommend and will impact performance as it requires the task queue be recreated. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.simulationWorkers.

Controls if bodies go to sleep when not moving and not interacting. Sleeping can provide a significant performance improvement when many Dynamic or Kinematic bodies are in the world. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.sleepingAllowed
Controls the transform plane that the world uses when writing transforms. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.transformWriteMode. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.transformPlane.

Controls if Transform tweening is used. Transform tweening is where bodies that have their LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.transformObject set, write to the UnityEngine.Transform each frame depending on the specific body LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.TransformWriteMode set. Regardless of this setting, Transform tweening is never used if the LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.simulationType set to LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.SimulationType.Update or LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.transformWriteMode is LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.TransformWriteMode.Off. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.transformTweening.

Controls how transform writing is handled. Only bodies that have their LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.transformWriteMode active and produce a LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsEvents.BodyUpdateEvent will write to a transform. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld.TransformWriteMode.
Create a default LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsWorld definition.

member.param: Controls whether the default settings come from the physics settings or not.

summary.para: The geometry of a closed convex polygon. See LowLevelPhysics2D.PhysicsBody.CreateShape.

summary.para: Create multiple Polygons from a set of vertices. The vertices are assumed to produce a closed loop but can describe a concave shape if required. There must be at least 3 vertices. A limit is imposed on small vertex distances so it is recommended that scaling is applied here rather than on the returned geometry so geometry is not discarded due to it being invalid.

member.param: The vertices to create the polygons from..
The transform used to specify where the geometry is positioned.
The scaling to be applied to the vertices.
The memory allocator to use for the results. This can only be Unity.Collections.Allocator.Temp, Unity.Collections.Allocator.TempJob or Unity.Collections.Allocator.Persistent.

returns.para: The created polygon geometries. This NativeArray must be disposed of after use otherwise leaks will occur. The exception to this is if the array is empty.
